subject: Warp hiccups on Nvidia a6000 with cu11.4
body:
Hi,
Has anyone on this group tried to run WArp with the latest/greatest
Nvidia GPU's and willing to share their experience?
I am trying to benchmark our dedicated Warp/M machine that has 2x
A6000 with 48GB of memory each and it is loaded with CUDA 11.4 and
driver version 472.93. While the Warp part of the pipeline was fairly
snappy (2mins/tilt series with 2 GPUs), M is really struggling (the
pathology is stuttering progress and the program window freezing)
especially at the denoising step of the species generation. I suspect
that this is most likely due to the CUDA drivers and the special
version of TensorFlow that Warp ships with and the resulting
incompatibility.
I'd be happy to hear from anyone who has a similar computer setup as
ours and learn from their experience in setting up the machine to work
optimally with Warp/M.

Best,
Pranav
--
Pranav Shah
Postdoctoral Research Fellow.

Division of Structural Biology,
Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
University of Oxford,
Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
UK
